description: Projected 13-16 seeds, auto-bid mid-majors, and bubble teams with upset potential for the 2026 NCAA Tournament

meta.cinderella_landscape: NIL and transfer portal have widened the talent gap between power conferences and mid-majors. 2025 tournament produced only 13 underdog wins (tied fewest since 1985 field expansion). Three straight tournaments without multiple double-digit seeds in Sweet 16. Experts predict another chalk year in 2026.
meta.key_insight: Continuity and coaching experience are the strongest predictors of mid-major upset potential. Teams with returning cores and experienced tournament coaches are the most dangerous lower seeds.

teams.auto_bid_status: Lock for NCAA tournament regardless of MAC tournament result

coach.name: Travis Steele
coach.tournament_experience: Former Xavier head coach; first NCAA tournament with Miami OH
coach.style_notes: Emphasizes passing, fast pace, five-out alignment creating paint pressure for kick-out 3s. Modernized offense dramatically — scoring average jumped from 70.6 to 80.6 to 90.9 PPG over three seasons.

key_players.name: Peter Suder
key_players.notes: Team leader, versatile scorer and facilitator

key_players.name: Eian Elmer
key_players.notes: Career-high 32 points in regular season finale OT win vs Ohio

strengths: Undefeated record — first D1 team to finish regular season 31-0 with 90+ PPG, 50%+ FG, 10+ threes/game
Elite offensive efficiency — #1 nationally in FG%, #2 in scoring
Five balanced scorers in double figures
Deep roster with high-scoring bench
Extreme confidence from undefeated run
Fast tempo creates havoc for opponents unfamiliar with pace
weaknesses: KenPom SOS ranked #282 — extremely weak schedule
NET ranking only 55th despite 31-0 record
Multiple power conference teams REFUSED to schedule them
Defensive efficiency mediocre — 215th nationally in opponent PPG
No quality wins against top-50 opponents
Untested against elite competition
cinderella_factors.historical_context: Third D1 program to finish regular season undefeated (after 2013-14 Wichita State, 2015-16 North Carolina). First MAC team to go undefeated in conference play.
cinderella_factors.upset_narrative: The undefeated story will generate massive public attention and media coverage. If they draw a beatable 5/6 seed, the pressure will be on the favorite. However, the massive step up in competition quality is the real concern.
cinderella_factors.overall_assessment: Historic season but serious questions about competition level. Their offense is legit elite, but they haven't faced the kind of athleticism and defensive pressure that power conference teams bring. A classic trap game for a higher seed — massive public backing, no fear of losing.

notable_wins: 110-108 OT win vs Ohio to complete undefeated regular season
teams.program_history: Limited NCAA tournament history. This would be a landmark appearance.
teams.style_of_play: Up-tempo, high-scoring, five-out offense with emphasis on 3-point shooting and ball movement. Top of D1 in assists. Play fast and shoot efficiently.

teams.auto_bid_status: Bubble — needs AAC tournament success for at-large or auto-bid

coach.name: Eric Konkol
coach.tournament_experience: Fourth-year head coach at Tulsa. Limited NCAA tournament head coaching experience.
coach.style_notes: Strong non-conference start (13-1). Solid but not spectacular in AAC play.

key_stats.notes: Started 7-0 in December 2025, 6-1 in November. 90-56 blowout win over Tulane on Feb 25.

strengths: Strong overall record at 23-6
Blowout capability shown in conference play
NET ranking inside top 50
weaknesses: AAC is mid-tier conference
Limited data on quality wins against ranked opponents
May need auto-bid to make tournament
cinderella_factors.overall_assessment: Solid mid-major with a good record but unclear ceiling. Needs AAC tournament run to punch ticket.

teams.program_history: Tulsa has NCAA tournament history including a Final Four in 2000. Program has been up and down but has institutional tournament DNA.
teams.style_of_play: Balanced offensive approach. Can blow out weaker opponents.

teams.auto_bid_status: At-large likely — wins above bubble score of 3.49, strong quad 1 wins

coach.name: Johnny Dawkins
coach.tournament_experience: Tenth-year head coach. Limited NCAA tournament success at UCF.
coach.style_notes: Has elevated UCF's profile in Big 12. Best NET ranking since joining the conference.

key_stats.notes: Best NET ranking (as high as 30th) since joining Big 12

strengths: Five quad 1 wins including victories over Texas A&M (sweep), Kansas, Colorado, Texas Tech, BYU
Battle-tested in Big 12 — plays elite competition weekly
NET ranking has been as high as 30th
Strong wins above bubble metric
weaknesses: 10 losses show inconsistency
Not a traditional mid-major — Big 12 team, just lower-seeded
Could draw a tough matchup
cinderella_factors.overall_assessment: Not a classic Cinderella — they are a power conference team. But as a lower seed, their Big 12 experience means they won't be intimidated. Dangerous 8-10 seed.

teams.program_history: UCF has limited NCAA tournament history but is building under Dawkins in the Big 12.
teams.style_of_play: Competitive in Big 12 play with ability to upset ranked opponents. Strong road performance.

teams.auto_bid_status: #2 seed in MAC tournament — needs auto-bid (Miami OH is #1)

coach.style_notes: Set program record for regular season wins (26). 31 consecutive home wins at James A. Rhodes Arena.

key_stats.notes: Shot 66.7% from deep in 94-55 blowout of NIU. 31 consecutive home wins.

strengths: Program-record 26 regular season wins
Dominant home court — 31 consecutive wins
Only 1 MAC loss — elite in conference
Elite 3-point shooting capability
weaknesses: Miami OH is the MAC favorite for auto-bid
Would need to beat undefeated Miami OH in MAC tournament for auto-bid
MAC schedule strength concerns
NET ranking outside top 50
cinderella_factors.overall_assessment: Very good MAC team but plays second fiddle to Miami OH. If they upset Miami in MAC tournament, they'd be a dangerous low seed with a chip on their shoulder. Can shoot the lights out from 3.

notable_wins: 94-55 blowout of NIU with 66.7% from three
teams.program_history: Akron has made the NCAA tournament several times and had a few first-round wins historically.
teams.style_of_play: High-volume 3-point shooting, strong home court advantage.

teams.notes: These two teams are the MAC's best — if Miami OH wins the auto-bid, Akron likely stays home. If Akron upsets undefeated Miami, the story flips: Miami OH gets an at-large bid (likely) and Akron gets the auto-bid. Either way, at least one MAC team makes the tournament.

teams.auto_bid_status: Deep bubble — needs SEC tournament run to make NCAA tournament

coach.name: Porter Moser
coach.tournament_experience: Led Loyola-Chicago to Final Four in 2018. Experienced tournament coach.
coach.style_notes: Defensive-minded approach. Struggled in loaded SEC.

key_stats.notes: 12th in SEC. 6-11 conference record is concerning.

strengths: Porter Moser's tournament pedigree (Loyola Final Four)
SEC experience means they've played elite competition
Brand name program
weaknesses: 16-14 record is unimpressive
Sub-.500 in conference play
Unlikely to make tournament without SEC tournament run
Not playing well enough to be dangerous
cinderella_factors.overall_assessment: A struggling power conference team, not a traditional Cinderella. Unlikely to make the field. If they somehow get in via SEC tournament run, Moser's experience gives them a floor, but ceiling is low.

teams.program_history: Historic program with multiple Final Four appearances. But current team is underperforming.
teams.style_of_play: Defensive-oriented but not executing at a high level in 2025-26.

teams.auto_bid_status: Bubble — needs Big East tournament success or strong finish

coach.name: Shaheen Holloway
coach.tournament_experience: Fourth-year head coach. Led Saint Peter's to Elite Eight as a 15-seed in 2022 (as coach). Known for tournament magic.
coach.style_notes: Massive turnaround from 7-25 last season to 20-11. Defensive identity.

key_stats.notes: Remarkable improvement from 7-25 (2024-25) to 20-11 (2025-26). 4th in Big East.

key_players.name: Stephon Payne
key_players.notes: 22 rebounds in win at Marquette — elite rebounder

key_players.name: Najai Hines
key_players.notes: Key bench contributor, 12 pts/10 reb vs Georgetown

strengths: Shaheen Holloway has PROVEN tournament magic (Saint Peter's 15-seed Elite Eight run)
Massive year-over-year improvement shows team is peaking
Big East competition has prepared them well
Strong rebounding presence (Payne's 22-rebound game)
4th in Big East is respectable
weaknesses: 10-10 in conference isn't dominant
Bubble status means they may not make the field
Young program chemistry after major roster overhaul
cinderella_factors.overall_assessment: If Seton Hall makes the tournament, Holloway is the kind of coach who can manufacture upsets. His Saint Peter's run proves he knows how to prepare for the tournament. The turnaround from 7-25 to 20-11 is remarkable and suggests a team trending in the right direction at the right time.

notable_wins: Competitive games against St. John's and UConn in Big East
teams.program_history: Won the 1989 national championship. Multiple Big East titles. Tournament program historically.
teams.style_of_play: Physical, defensive-minded. Strong rebounding. Coach known for opponent-specific game planning.

teams.auto_bid_status: Bubble — needs Mountain West tournament run

key_stats.notes: 16-11 as of early March. Voted preseason 3rd in Mountain West behind San Diego State and Utah State.

strengths: Physical style of play that can bother higher seeds
Experienced roster
Identified by analysts as Cinderella candidate (11-seed in South per projections)
Final season in Mountain West — extra motivation
weaknesses: Mediocre record for a tournament team
Mountain West schedule strength is middling
Preseason expectations of 3rd — may have underperformed
cinderella_factors.overall_assessment: Identified by multiple outlets as a potential Cinderella. Physical play and experienced roster make them a tough out, but need to make the field first.

teams.program_history: Limited NCAA tournament history. Moving to Pac-12 after this season.
teams.style_of_play: Physical, defensive-minded. Can grind out games.

teams.auto_bid_status: Bubble — 7th seed in Big 12 tournament with first-round bye

coach.name: Ross Hodge
coach.tournament_experience: First-year head coach at WVU (fourth head coach in four years). Previously at North Texas.
coach.style_notes: Exceeded preseason expectations — finished 4 spots higher than preseason poll. Dealing with massive coaching instability (4 coaches in 4 years).

key_stats.notes: 9-9 in Big 12 is respectable given coaching turmoil. Won tiebreakers for 7th seed due to series sweeps.

strengths: Big 12 battle-tested — plays elite competition
Exceeded preseason expectations significantly
Won tiebreakers showing clutch play
First-round bye in Big 12 tournament
weaknesses: Four head coaches in four years — institutional instability
.500 in conference play
13 losses is a lot for a tournament team
First-year coach with no WVU tournament experience
cinderella_factors.overall_assessment: Big 12 experience is valuable but 18-13 is borderline. If they make the tournament, they've seen the best competition in the country. Coaching instability is a wildcard — could rally around underdog narrative or fall apart.

teams.program_history: Historic program under Bob Huggins. Multiple Final Four appearances. Strong tournament tradition.
teams.style_of_play: Physical Big 12 style. 3-point shooting is key on both ends.

coach.name: Kyle Smith
coach.tournament_experience: Limited NCAA tournament head coaching experience at Stanford.
coach.style_notes: Has a star player in Ebuka Okorie who can take over games.

key_stats.notes: 7th in ACC. Close losses in ACC tournament (75-73 buzzer beater loss to Louisville).

key_players.name: Ebuka Okorie
key_players.notes: Season-high 40 points vs Georgia Tech. 36 points and 9 assists vs UNC. 33 points vs NC State in regular season finale. Potential NBA prospect with scoring explosions.

strengths: Ebuka Okorie is a potential game-changer — 40-point scoring ceiling
ACC experience against top competition
Beat UNC 95-90 at home
Capable of high-scoring games (95+ points multiple times)
weaknesses: Inconsistent — 12 losses
Heartbreaking buzzer-beater ACC tournament loss may affect morale
Reliant on one star player
cinderella_factors.overall_assessment: If Okorie is on, Stanford can beat anyone. His 40-point ceiling makes them a tough draw for any seed. But consistency is the question — when he's off, they're an average team.

teams.program_history: Stanford has reached the Final Four (1998) and made multiple tournament appearances.
teams.style_of_play: Offensive-minded with heavy 3-point shooting. Star-driven system around Okorie.

teams.auto_bid_status: At-large likely — 7th seed in SEC tournament, projected 6-seed in NCAA

coach.name: Dennis Gates
coach.tournament_experience: Fourth-year head coach. Won Big House Gaines Award. Led Mizzou to NCAA tournament previously.
coach.style_notes: Emphasizes versatility and frontcourt depth. Strong at home against ranked opponents.

key_stats.notes: Tied for 6th in SEC. Beat Mississippi State 85-73 in SEC tournament before losing to Florida 95-81.

key_players.name: Jevon Porter
key_players.notes: Transfer from Loyola Marymount. Key frontcourt addition.

strengths: SEC experience — 16 of 18 conference games against 2025 NCAA tournament teams
Gates is experienced tournament coach
Strong home schedule builds toughness
Frontcourt versatility with transfer additions
weaknesses: 12 losses in loaded SEC
Lost to Drake 67-57 in first round of 2026 NCAA tournament (projected outcome per search results)
May be a first-round exit candidate
cinderella_factors.overall_assessment: Not a classic Cinderella — they're an SEC team that will be seeded 6-8. But the search results suggest they could be upset victims rather than upset makers. Drake's victory suggests Mizzou may underperform their seed.

notable_wins: Beat Mississippi State 85-73 in SEC tournament
teams.program_history: Elite Eight in 2023 under Gates. Program has Final Four history.
teams.style_of_play: Versatile, physical SEC team. Frontcourt-oriented with transfer portal additions.

teams.auto_bid_status: Needs Southland tournament auto-bid. 2nd seed in conference.

coach.name: Bill Armstrong
coach.tournament_experience: First-year head coach. Inherited strong program from Will Wade.
coach.style_notes: Maintained winning culture through coaching transition. Eight-game winning streak to end regular season.

key_stats.notes: Ended regular season on 8-game win streak. 127-65 blowout shows scoring potential.

key_players.name: Larry Johnson
key_players.notes: Scored 30 points in season high. 25 points to close regular season vs Nicholls. Lead scorer.

strengths: Won first-ever NCAA tournament game last year (beat Clemson 69-67 as 12-seed)
Tournament experience from 2025 run — players know what it takes
Peaking at the right time — 8-game win streak
Larry Johnson is a proven scorer who can take over games
Program built by Will Wade with strong culture
weaknesses: First-year coach navigating tournament pressure
Southland Conference competition level is low
Lost key players from last year's tournament team
2nd in conference — may not even get auto-bid
cinderella_factors.historical_context: Program's first-ever NCAA tournament win came in 2025 (beat Clemson). That experience is invaluable for a second straight run.
cinderella_factors.overall_assessment: McNeese proved last year they can compete in March. The question is whether a first-year coach can replicate that magic. If they make the field, their 2025 experience gives them an edge over typical Southland auto-bids.

notable_wins: Beat Clemson 69-67 in 2025 NCAA tournament (first-ever tournament win)
75-65 over Nicholls to close regular season
teams.program_history: First NCAA tournament win ever in 2025. Building a mid-major powerhouse under Will Wade's foundation.
teams.style_of_play: Guard-driven offense. Can score in bunches (127 points in one game). Physical play.

teams.auto_bid_status: Lost in MVC tournament quarterfinals to 9-seed Drake 100-81. Needs at-large bid (unlikely from MVC).

coach.name: Casey Alexander
coach.tournament_experience: Seventh-year head coach. Belmont has made 9 NCAA tournament appearances historically.
coach.style_notes: One of only 4 programs to win 20+ games in 15 consecutive seasons (with Gonzaga, Kansas, Oregon).

key_stats.notes: No. 1 seed in MVC tournament but upset by 9-seed Drake — first time a 9-seed beat a 1-seed in MVC tournament history.

key_players.name: Aidan Noyes
key_players.notes: 19 points in MVC tournament. Key contributor.

strengths: Elite regular season record (26-5)
One of only 4 programs with 15 consecutive 20-win seasons
9 NCAA tournament appearances — program knows how to compete in March
Balanced scoring attack
MVC is a respected mid-major conference
weaknesses: Lost MVC tournament as 1-seed to 9-seed Drake — devastating upset
At-large bid from MVC is extremely difficult to earn
Lost Nic McClain to season-ending injury
Tournament loss raises questions about composure under pressure
cinderella_factors.overall_assessment: Belmont had a fantastic regular season but the MVC tournament loss likely ends their NCAA tournament hopes. If they somehow get an at-large bid, their 26-5 record and tournament pedigree make them dangerous. But this is probably NIT territory.

teams.program_history: 9 NCAA tournament appearances. Known as a mid-major powerhouse under Rick Byrd's legacy. Belmont is synonymous with March Madness upsets.
teams.style_of_play: Efficient offensive team. Balanced scoring. Strong shooting program historically.

teams.auto_bid_status: Regular season champion. Unanimous preseason favorite. Heavy favorite to win Ivy League tournament (March 13-15 at Cornell).

coach.name: James Jones
coach.tournament_experience: 26th-year head coach. Multiple Ivy League titles. Has led Yale to NCAA tournament multiple times including 2-seed upset wins.
coach.style_notes: Master of preparation. Gets the most out of academic-focused rosters.

key_stats.notes: Clinched second straight Ivy League title. Unanimously voted preseason favorite.

key_players.name: Nick Townsend
key_players.notes: First Team All-Ivy. Team captain. 4th in Ivy League in scoring, 3rd in rebounding. Double-double machine.

key_players.name: Samson Aletan
key_players.notes: Elite rim protector. One of the best shot-blockers in mid-major basketball. Junior anchor.

strengths: James Jones is in his 26th year — master tactician who prepares meticulously
Back-to-back Ivy League regular season and tournament champions
Nick Townsend is a legitimate star (16.9 PPG, 7.6 RPG)
Samson Aletan provides elite rim protection rarely seen in Ivy League
Yale has upset history — beat Baylor as a 12-seed in 2016
Continuity — no transfer portal churn, players develop together for 4 years
Smart players who execute game plans and don't beat themselves
weaknesses: Ivy League competition level is among the lowest in D1
Limited athleticism compared to power conference teams
Can't match the depth of higher-seeded opponents
Three losses show vulnerability
cinderella_factors.historical_context: Yale upset Baylor as a 12-seed in 2016 NCAA tournament. The Ivy League has produced upsets historically (Harvard, Princeton). Yale's recent consistency makes them a perennial threat.
cinderella_factors.overall_assessment: Yale is the classic Cinderella template — smart, well-coached, experienced players who play together for years. Townsend is a legitimate star, and Aletan's rim protection can neutralize opposing big men. Jones will have a perfect game plan. If they get a 13 or 14 seed, they are ABSOLUTELY capable of a first-round upset.

notable_wins: Second straight Ivy League regular season title
teams.program_history: Yale beat Baylor as 12-seed in 2016. Multiple Ivy League titles under James Jones. Program is at its strongest in decades.
teams.style_of_play: Disciplined, half-court offense. Strong interior defense with Aletan. Smart ball movement. Don't turn it over. Execute in late-game situations.

teams.auto_bid_status: 4th in Mountain West. Needs conference tournament run or at-large (unlikely).

coach.name: Bryce Drew
coach.tournament_experience: 121-40 at GCU. Three straight WAC tournament titles. Three straight NCAA tournament appearances. THE premier mid-major tournament coach.
coach.style_notes: High-scoring offense. Dynamic guard play. Won three straight conference tournaments — knows how to peak in March.

key_stats.notes: First season in Mountain West after moving from WAC. 4th place finish is solid for first year in stronger conference.

key_players.name: Rayquan Johnson
key_players.notes: Dynamic scorer. Identified by analysts as key to Cinderella potential.

strengths: Bryce Drew is a PROVEN March coach — three straight conference tournament titles, three straight NCAA bids
Drew himself hit the famous buzzer-beater for Valparaiso in 1998 tournament — March is in his DNA
High-scoring offense can run with anyone
Dynamic guard play creates matchup problems
Identified by Bleacher Report and other outlets as a top Cinderella candidate
Strong rebounding team
Four straight NCAA tournament appearances (including current season projection)
weaknesses: First year in Mountain West — adjustment period
11 losses show inconsistency in stronger conference
May not make the field if they don't win MW tournament
Lost some key players from WAC championship runs
cinderella_factors.historical_context: Bryce Drew hit one of the most iconic shots in NCAA tournament history for Valparaiso in 1998. He brings that March magic as a coach — three straight conference tournament titles at GCU.
cinderella_factors.overall_assessment: If GCU makes the tournament, they are arguably the MOST DANGEROUS Cinderella candidate. Drew's tournament DNA (as player AND coach), high-scoring offense, and dynamic guard play make them a nightmare matchup for a higher seed. Multiple outlets have identified them as their top upset pick.

notable_wins: Three straight WAC tournament titles (previous years)
Competitive in Mountain West debut season
teams.program_history: Three straight NCAA tournament appearances. Program has risen rapidly under Bryce Drew. Transitioned from D2 to D1 in 2013.
teams.style_of_play: Up-tempo, guard-driven, high-scoring offense. Can shoot the three and attack the rim. Drew's teams play with confidence and freedom in March.

teams.auto_bid_status: CLINCHED — Won Big South tournament 91-76 over Winthrop. Second consecutive auto-bid.

coach.name: Flynn Clayman
coach.tournament_experience: First-year head coach. Promoted from associate HC after Alan Huss left for Creighton. Won Big South tournament in first season.
coach.style_notes: Won with only ONE returning starter and nine new rotation players. Remarkable coaching job. Defensive-first identity — nation's leader in steal percentage.

key_stats.notes: Third consecutive Big South regular season championship. Won conference tournament 91-76. 27-4 overall. Nation's leader in steal percentage.

key_players.name: Rob Martin
key_players.notes: Transfer from SE Missouri State. 2-year starter. Lead scorer despite small stature.

strengths: Nation's leader in steal percentage — creates havoc on defense
Balanced scoring attack with deep rotation
Won Big South tournament with only ONE returning starter — shows incredible coaching and team chemistry
Third consecutive conference regular season title — program excellence
91-76 dominant tournament championship win
Second consecutive NCAA tournament appearance builds program confidence
weaknesses: NET ranking of 75 is low — will likely be a 15 or 16 seed
Big South is one of the weakest conferences in D1
First-year head coach in NCAA tournament
Only one returning starter — limited tournament experience on roster
Small guard (5-10 Rob Martin) may struggle against bigger teams
cinderella_factors.historical_context: High Point's second ever NCAA tournament appearance (first was 2025). Program is building something special under former coach Huss's foundation.
cinderella_factors.overall_assessment: High Point is a feel-good story — first-year coach, one returning starter, three straight conference titles. Their defensive identity (nation's best steal percentage) can create chaos. As a 15/16 seed they'd need everything to go right, but they have the defensive disruption capability to make a game interesting.

teams.program_history: Second NCAA tournament appearance ever (both in 2025 and 2026). Rapidly ascending program.
teams.style_of_play: Defensive havoc — nation's leader in steal percentage. Creates turnovers and runs in transition. Balanced offensive attack with deep bench.

cinderella_rankings.description: Ranked by upset potential if they make the 2026 NCAA tournament

tier_1_most_dangerous.reason: Bryce Drew's March DNA (player and coach), high-scoring offense, dynamic guards. Top Cinderella pick by multiple national outlets.
31-0 undefeated record, #1 in FG%, #2 in scoring nationally. Historic offense. Massive media narrative. Untested but lethal if offense translates.

tier_2_dangerous.reason: 26th-year coach James Jones, star player Townsend (16.9 PPG), elite rim protector Aletan, Ivy League upset history. Classic Cinderella template.
Won first-ever tournament game last year (beat Clemson). Tournament experience from 2025 run. Peaking with 8-game win streak.
Shaheen Holloway's Saint Peter's 15-seed Elite Eight pedigree. Massive 7-25 to 20-11 turnaround. Big East battle-tested.

tier_3_capable.reason: Nation's best steal percentage. Havoc defense. But likely 15/16 seed facing enormous talent gap.
Five quad 1 wins in Big 12. Not a classic mid-major but dangerous as lower power conference seed.
26-5 with elite 3-point shooting. Program-record wins. But may not make field.
Ebuka Okorie's 40-point ceiling can beat anyone on his night. Inconsistent otherwise.

tier_4_unlikely.reason: 26-5 but lost MVC tournament to 9-seed Drake. Probably NIT-bound.
Physical team but mediocre record. Needs MW tournament run.
Big 12 tested but 18-13 and coaching instability.
SEC team likely seeded 6-8 — more likely to be upset than to upset.
Solid record but limited info on quality wins.
16-14 in SEC. Unlikely to make field.

key_narratives_for_simulation.nil_impact: NIL and transfer portal have widened talent gap. Experts predict another chalk year. Only 13 underdog wins in 2025 (tied for fewest since 1985). Three straight years without multiple double-digit seeds in Sweet 16.
key_narratives_for_simulation.coaching_matters_most: In the Cinderella era of NIL, the most dangerous lower seeds have elite tournament coaches: Bryce Drew (GCU), James Jones (Yale), Shaheen Holloway (Seton Hall), Travis Steele (Miami OH).
key_narratives_for_simulation.undefeated_miami: Miami OH's 31-0 record will dominate public brackets. KenPom SOS of 282 means they're potentially overvalued by the public and undervalued by metrics. This creates a massive divergence between public perception and statistical reality.
key_narratives_for_simulation.simulation_implications: Lower seed upset probabilities should be DECREASED from historical averages given the NIL talent consolidation trend. But apply coaching and experience bonuses to teams with proven March coaches (Drew, Jones, Holloway). Miami OH's offensive efficiency metrics should carry extra weight despite weak SOS.
